Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BRETT T. MORONEY, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendants Mary Seibel, Omid Kordestani, and Gisel Kordestani;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ADNAAN KHAN's Motion For Order To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery; And For Monetary Sanctions.

Defendants stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendants (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion to compel GRANTED in part. From the reply, the only interrogatory still at issue is Form Interrogatory 12.3. The California Supreme Court held that witness statements may be protected work product but requires a showing to support such a claim. Coito v. Superior Court (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, 495-97. Here, Defendant's separate statement said that she only knew about her deposition and the deposition of plaintiff and agreed to amend her response accordingly. The amended supplemental response instead said Defendant was "not aware of any other nonprivileged written or recorded statements." If there are statements that are withheld based on work-product, Defendant did not make a sufficient showing. If there are no such statements, Defendant should remove the reference to nonprivileged statements because it introduces ambiguity. The requests for sanctions are DENIED. It is apparent that further meet-and-confer efforts could have resolved this motion.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
